title: erigeron tephropodus
intro: Erigeron tephropodus, also known as the mountain fleabane, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. Erigeron tephropodus is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, such as colds, fever, and skin conditions.
